title: Rational coefficient combinatorics
summary: Defines factorial, binomial, trinomial, rising-factorial, and falling-factorial expressions over the rationals and proves their zero, successor, and predecessor product identities.

mathematicalContent.heading: Rationalized combinatorics
mathematicalContent.body: Natural factorials and binomial coefficients are embedded in ℚ so they can be combined with rational OR weight parameters.

mathematicalContent.heading: Pochhammer symbols
mathematicalContent.body: The rising and falling factorials are finite products; the module proves their recursive last-factor decompositions.

mathematicalContent.heading: Verified arithmetic
mathematicalContent.body: All the displayed successor and positive-index predecessor formulas are proved directly in Lean, without geometric interfaces.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Casts the natural factorial m! into the rational numbers.
Casts the binomial coefficient C(m,j) into the rational numbers.

correspondence.leanType: def multinomialQ3 (N a b c : ℕ) : ℚ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the rational trinomial factor N!/(a!b!c!).
Defines the rising factorial, or Pochhammer symbol.
Proves that the rational factorial at zero is one.

correspondence.leanType: theorem factorialQ_succ (m : ℕ) : factorialQ (m + 1) = ((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℚ) * factorialQ m
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves the factorial successor identity after casting to ℚ.

correspondence.leanType: theorem binomQ_self (m : ℕ) : binomQ m m = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that the length-zero rising factorial is one.

correspondence.leanType: theorem pochhammerQ_succ (x : ℚ) (m : ℕ) : pochhammerQ x (m + 1) = pochhammerQ x m * (x + (m : ℚ))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that the length-zero falling factorial is one.

correspondence.leanType: theorem fallingFactorialQ_succ (x : ℚ) (m : ℕ) : fallingFactorialQ x (m + 1) = fallingFactorialQ x m * (x - (m : ℚ))
theorem pochhammerQ_eq_pred_mul (x : ℚ) {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) : pochhammerQ x m = pochhammerQ x (m - 1) * (x + ((m - 1 : ℕ) : ℚ))
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For m>0, isolates the final factor in a rising factorial.

correspondence.leanType: theorem fallingFactorialQ_eq_pred_mul (x : ℚ) {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) : fallingFactorialQ x m = fallingFactorialQ x (m - 1) * (x - ((m - 1 : ℕ) : ℚ))
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For m>0, isolates the final factor in a falling factorial.

references.locator: Classification of invariant bilinear differential operators
references.note: Flat-model origin of the coefficient family.
Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: These are exact rational combinatorial identities. They do not assert that any particular OR coefficient system satisfies a geometric recurrence or produces a tangential operator.
